context:
Section 7 (Conclusions), page 27.

Nevertheless, there are many other unresolved stability issues in NEPs. For instance, is the I-property also necessary for the isolated calmness of \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\)? In the context of NLP, the answer is positive, where the I-property actually reduces to the SOSC (Dontchev and Rockafellar [9]) under the SONC for optimality.
title: Test necessity of I-property for isolated calmness of KKT mapping
background_latex:
Consider a Nash equilibrium problem (NEP) with canonical perturbations. There are \(N\) players. Player \(k\) chooses a strategy \(x_k\in\mathbb{R}^{n_k}\) and faces constraints depending only on \(x_k\): for indices \(i=1,\dots,s_k\) (equalities) and \(j=s_k+1,\dots,m_k\) (inequalities),
\[
 g_i^k(x_k;w_k)=u_i^k,\qquad i=1,\dots,s_k,\qquad \text{and}\qquad g_j^k(x_k;w_k)\le u_j^k,\qquad j=s_k+1,\dots,m_k,
\]
where \(u^k\in\mathbb{R}^{m_k}\) and \(w_k\in\mathbb{R}^{d_k}\) are perturbation parameters. The objective of player \(k\) is
\[
 x_k\mapsto f_k(x_k,x_{-k};w_k)-\langle v_k,x_k\rangle,
\]
with \(v_k\in\mathbb{R}^{n_k}\) another perturbation parameter and \(x_{-k}\) denoting the strategies of all other players. Let \(p_k=(u^k,v_k,w_k)\) and \(p=(p_1,\dots,p_N)\).

A (local) Nash equilibrium \(\bar x=(\bar x_1,\dots,\bar x_N)\) at \(\bar p\) is a strategy profile such that each \(\bar x_k\) is a (local) minimizer of player \(k\)'s problem when other players are fixed at \(\bar x_{-k}\).

Let \(\lambda_k\in\mathbb{R}^{m_k}\) be the Lagrange multipliers for player \(k\)'s constraints, with \(\lambda_{k,i}\) free for equalities \(i\le s_k\) and \(\lambda_{k,j}\ge 0\) for inequalities \(j>s_k\). Define the Lagrangian
\[
L_k(x_k,x_{-k},\lambda_k;w_k):= f_k(x_k,x_{-k};w_k)+\sum_{i=1}^{m_k}\lambda_{k,i} g_i^k(x_k;w_k).
\]
The KKT solution mapping \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\) associates to each perturbation \(p\) the set of KKT pairs \((x,\lambda)\) satisfying the KKT conditions for all players simultaneously (stationarity in \(x_k\), primal feasibility, complementary slackness, and dual feasibility).

For a given reference KKT triple \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\in\mathrm{gph}\,S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\), define for each player \(k\) the standard active-set partition of inequality constraints at \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\):
1) \(I_1^k\): equalities and inequalities active with strictly positive multiplier;
2) \(I_2^k\): active inequalities with zero multiplier;
3) \(I_3^k\): inactive inequalities.
Let \(I_1:=\bigcup_k I_1^k\) and \(I_2:=\bigcup_k I_2^k\). Define the cone
\[
K(I_1,I_2):=\Bigl\{y=(y_1,\dots,y_N):\ \nabla_{x_k} g_i^k(\bar x_k;\bar w_k)^\top y_k=0\ \forall i\in I_1^k,\ \nabla_{x_k} g_i^k(\bar x_k;\bar w_k)^\top y_k\le 0\ \forall i\in I_2^k\Bigr\}.
\]
The paper defines the I-property at \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) on \(K(I_1,I_2)\) as the implication: if \(y\in K(I_1,I_2)\) satisfies, for each \(k=1,\dots,N\),
\[
\sum_{i=1}^N y_k^\top \nabla^2_{x_k x_i} L_k(\bar x_k,\bar x_{-k},\bar\lambda_k;\bar w_k)\, y_i = 0,
\]
then \(y=0\).

The isolated calmness of \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\) at \(\bar p\) for \((\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) is the property that there exist neighborhoods \(U\) of \(\bar p\) and \(V\) of \((\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) and a constant \(\kappa\ge 0\) such that
\[
S_{\mathrm{KKT}}(p)\cap V\subset \{(\bar x,\bar\lambda)\}+\kappa\|p-\bar p\|B\quad \text{for all }p\in U,
\]
where \(B\) is the unit ball. (Robust isolated calmness additionally requires \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}(p)\cap V\neq\emptyset\) for all \(p\in U\).)

The paper proves that, assuming a strict Mangasarian--Fromovitz constraint qualification (SMFCQ) for each player at \((\bar p_k,\bar x_k,\bar\lambda_k)\), the I-property is sufficient for isolated calmness of \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\) at \(\bar p\) for \((\bar x,\bar\lambda)\).
problem_definition_latex:
Determine whether the I-property is a necessary condition for the isolated calmness of the KKT solution mapping \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\) at \(\bar p\) for \((\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) in Nash equilibrium problems with canonical perturbations.

Concretely: under the same baseline regularity assumptions used to study isolated calmness in this setting (in particular, assuming the strict Mangasarian--Fromovitz constraint qualification holds for each player at \((\bar p_k,\bar x_k,\bar\lambda_k)\)), decide whether
\[
S_{\mathrm{KKT}} \text{ is isolated calm at } \bar p \text{ for }(\bar x,\bar\lambda)
\]
implies that Problem (1.2) satisfies the I-property at \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) on \(K(I_1,I_2)\).
latex:

Concretely: under the same baseline regularity assumptions used to study isolated calmness in this setting (in particular, assuming the strict Mangasarian--Fromovitz constraint qualification holds for each player at \((\bar p_k,\bar x_k,\bar\lambda_k)\)), decide whether

\]
implies that Problem (1.2) satisfies the I-property at \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) on \(K(I_1,I_2)\).
open_problem_quote: Nevertheless, there are many other unresolved stability issues in NEPs. For instance, is the I-property also necessary for the isolated calmness of \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\)? In the context of NLP, the answer is positive, where the I-property actually reduces to the SOSC (Dontchev and Rockafellar [9]) under the SONC for optimality.
subject_classification: Variational analysis and stability of Nash equilibrium problems
counterexample_suitability_rationale: A counterexample would require constructing a smooth multi-player NEP where isolated calmness holds but the I-property fails; this is a structured but nontrivial existence question. Automated search could explore small-dimensional polynomial/quadratic games with simple constraints and test isolated calmness numerically via local solution mapping behavior, but certifying isolated calmness (a set-valued Lipschitz property) is delicate.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: While candidate instances (games) can be parameterized and KKT systems solved numerically, an automatic and reliable fitness signal for isolated calmness versus failure is hard to compute at scale and to certify. Evaluating the I-property is straightforward via linear-algebraic checks once derivatives are known, but verifying isolated calmness robustly would likely need symbolic/variational certification beyond routine automated loops.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.
